name: Zaporizhzhia National University (ZNU)
description: ZNU is a leading education and research center in Zaporizhzhia region, ensuring continuity and operational stability under wartime conditions. Founded in 1930, it is ranked TOP-10 in Ukraine based on national research assessment and 1st in Humanities and Arts Research category. The university has 12 faculties, an Engineering Educational and Scientific Institute, 4 professional colleges (including Metallurgical and Hydropower Colleges), 12,692 students, 1,248 staff, and 535 academic staff.
researchAreas: Green Metal Industry (high-entropy alloys, electrolyzers, catalytic gas purification)
Smart Grids and Renewable Energy Systems
Digital Twins for Industrial and Energy Systems
Green Industrial Transformation and Industrial Ecology

expertise: Metallurgy: Design and fabrication of high-entropy alloys, intermetallic compounds, and cermets under thermochemical pressing
Electrochemistry: Engineering of electrolyzer systems for magnesium extraction from fluoride-oxide melts for Al-Mg alloy production
Catalysis: Advancement of catalytic technologies for industrial gas purification from CO and hydrocarbons
Smart Grids: Enhancing energy resilience and real-time management of distributed renewables
Digital Twins: Modeling and optimizing industrial and energy systems through data-driven simulation
Renewable Energy: Advancing solar, wind, and hydropower for regional energy independence
AI/ML: Model development and optimization for industrial applications
HPC: Parallel and distributed computing for scientific simulations
STEM Education: Innovation in engineering and science pedagogy

trackRecord.internationalMobility: 30% academic staff have long-term mobility experience; 10% students with semester-long mobility

partnerships.organization: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
partnerships.description: Strategic partnership for Nansen EDU. Joint roadmap 2026-2028: Foundation → Pilots → Scaling. Focus areas: green metal industry, smart energy, digital twins, AI for industry.

warContext: Zaporizhzhia is a frontline region directly affected by the war. ZNU ensures continuity of education and research under wartime conditions through blended learning, mental and physical health support programs, institutional resilience measures (ESG strategy, solar power, Azure cloud migration), and VR-based interdisciplinary activities.

roadmapWithNTNU.2026: Foundation: Governance and clusters, Capacity/ESG/cybersecurity, Pilot concepts, EU proposals
roadmapWithNTNU.2027: Pilots: Applied pilots, Living Labs, Staff exchanges and training, Joint publications
roadmapWithNTNU.2028: Scaling: Regional pilots, Joint courses and ESG training, Co-supervised PhD/postdoc, Large-scale Horizon proposals
